Triggering the extreme cross-domain gene identification and the '10-person industrial service outsourcing team' demotion probe. This enterprise, named 'Ruichen Electronic Technology,' was classified by the official system as 'Electronic Device Manufacturing.' If one only looks at the first half of its license—'R&D, processing, and production of microwave equipment and accessories'—it indeed looks like a secretive, hardcore tech factory. However, the forensic logic engine astutely captured a highly bizarre mutation occurring in the latter half of its gene chain: 'lifting and packaging of mechanical equipment; domestic services; providing construction labor services.' The logical deduction instantly enters the highly grounded street-level workshop channel: a team with 10 employees and 400,000 paid-in capital, addressed in the Moling Industrial Park, is 'R&D microwave equipment' one second, and going off to do 'lifting, providing construction labor, and even domestic services' the next? In the physical world, this can absolutely not coexist within a high-tech electronics factory. The probe mercilessly deleted all padded rhetoric regarding 'R&D, production,' and locked onto its true actions that best fit the volume and logic of a 10-person industrial park team: 'lifting, packaging, and installation of mechanical equipment; providing construction labor services.' The truth is full of sweat and machine oil: this is absolutely not a microwave electronics manufacturing plant, but a pure 10-Employee Industrial Rigging & Electromechanical Labor Crew. The boss pulled together a 10-person team in the industrial park, primarily taking on equipment relocation (lifting, packaging) for other factories in the park, electromechanical equipment wiring and installation, or providing odd jobs in factories as a labor dispatch (even factory cleaning, which is the so-called 'domestic service'). The official 'Electronic Device Manufacturing' major factory tag has been thoroughly demoted by the system to the bottom-tier 'industrial labor and equipment loading/unloading team.'
